full_text:
Heatwave alert with storm, rain in Rajasthan:Mercury nears 46°C; Jaipur among districts set for weather shift, cloud cover likely
- Copy link
The heat in Rajasthan has started showing its intensity. On Saturday, the temperature reached close to 46 degrees Celsius. Areas of Barmer, Jaisalmer, and Jodhpur remained in the grip of a heatwave. Temperature was recorded above 40 degrees Celsius in several districts including Sri Ganganagar, Jalore, Bikaner, and Udaipur. In eastern districts Alwar, Jaipur, Karauli, and Dausa, the temperature remained below 40 degrees Celsius.
The Meteorological Department has issued orange and yellow alerts for severe heatwave conditions in western districts for the next two-three days. There is also a warning of dust storms, cloudy weather, and light rainfall in some places in eastern Rajasthan. Today (Sunday), a yellow alert for heatwave has been issued in 4 districts (Jaisalmer, Barmer, Jodhpur, Bikaner).
Pictures of weather in Rajasthan...
Rajasthan's hottest cities
Looking at the weather of the last 24 hours, the sky remained clear in most parts of the state and there was bright sunshine during the day. On Saturday (May 9), the hottest day was in Barmer, where the maximum temperature was recorded at 45.7 degrees Celsius. The maximum temperature was recorded at 45 in Jaisalmer and 44.8 degrees Celsius in Phalodi. All three areas experienced the effect of a heatwave from morning, which continued till late evening.
Apart from these districts, the heat remained intense in Chittorgarh, Jodhpur, Bikaner, Sri Ganganagar, and Jalore as well. On Saturday, the maximum temperature recorded was 43.8 degrees Celsius in Chittorgarh, 43.9 in Jodhpur, 42.2 in Bikaner, 41.1 in Jalore, and 41 degrees Celsius in Sri Ganganagar.
Temperature Below 40 in Eastern Rajasthan
While western districts experienced intense heat and heatwave, eastern districts got some relief from the severe heat. On Saturday, the maximum temperature was recorded below 40 degrees Celsius at several places including Sikar, Pilani (Jhunjhunu), Alwar, Tonk, Dausa, Karauli, and Fatehpur (Sikar). Light clouds appeared during the day around Kota district and light to moderate winds blew.
Jaipur: Humidity troubled people
In Jaipur on Saturday too, the temperature remained below 40 degrees Celsius. There was sunshine throughout the day in Jaipur, but the slight humidity in the afternoon troubled people. As evening arrived, a light breeze blew in Jaipur, bringing relief. The minimum temperature in Jaipur was recorded at 25.5 degrees Celsius. On May 11 and 12, there is a possibility of strong dust storms, cloudy skies, and scattered rain in some areas around Jaipur district.
Udaipur: Heat persisted even in the evening
The severe heat continued to affect Udaipur on Saturday as well. The city's maximum temperature was recorded at 41 degrees and minimum at 24.6 degrees Celsius. With the mercury staying above 40 degrees for the second consecutive day, the scorching sun and hot winds troubled people. Throughout the day, roads and markets saw less crowd compared to normal days.
Speaking of the evening, people come to walk at Mohanlal Sukhadia University campus and Constitution Park after four in the evening. Due to the heat, less movement is being observed at such places even in the evening.
Ajmer: Hot winds throughout the day caused trouble
The heat wave continued in Ajmer on Saturday as well. Everyone had to face trouble due to the hot winds and humidity throughout the day. The Meteorological Department recorded a maximum temperature of 40.2 degrees and a minimum temperature of 24.4 degrees on Saturday.
Sikar: Heat increased, scorching sun caused burns
In Sikar, bright sunshine appeared on Saturday morning due to clear skies. Light winds blew throughout the day. For the past week, the day temperature has been running below 40 degrees. People are seen protecting themselves from the scorching sun. At the weather center located at the Agricultural Research Center in Fatehpur in Sikar district, the maximum temperature during the day was recorded at 38.7 degrees and the minimum temperature in the morning was recorded at 22.7 degrees.
Alwar: Temperature still below 40 degrees
The heat was intense in Alwar on Saturday. The maximum temperature was around 38 degrees. The sun shone throughout the day. This made the heat felt continuously. However, due to the recent rains, the temperature has not risen much. Currently, the weather is clear. Light rain is possible in the next two days.
Kota: Temperature dropped by 2 degrees
The effect of heat continued in Kota on Saturday. The temperature dropped by up to 2 degrees, but the intense sun troubled people. Clouds appeared in the afternoon. The city's maximum temperature was recorded at 40.4 degrees and minimum at 25 degrees Celsius. In Kota, the maximum temperature has remained around 40 degrees for the past three days.
Jodhpur: Light clouds appeared
In Jodhpur on Saturday, the maximum daytime temperature was recorded at 43.9 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature remained at 30 degrees. Light clouds appeared in the city.
